description:
1. Start with your shoulders relaxed and your head in a neutral position. Slowly turn your head like you are looking over your shoulder.  
  2. Repeat to the other side.

Reps: 10-15 | Sets: 2-3 | Which Side: Both | Hold Time: 1-2 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: neck, shoulder, rotation, stretch, cervical, herniated disc, DDD, stenosis, neck rotation

description:
1. Sitting in a chair, make sure your back is straight and you are sitting upright. Place your finger on your chin.
  2. Move your chin away from your finger by moving your chin backwards and not tucking it in towards your chest. Hold the stretch.

Reps: 5-10 | Sets: 2-3 | Hold Time: 3-5 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: Chin, tucks, sitting, neck, shoulder, stretch, herniated disc, stenosis, text neck, posture

description:
1. On the side you want to stretch, bring your arm up and place your hand on the back of your shoulder blade so your elbow is pointing upward towards the ceiling. Take your other hand over your head and place it on the back of your head.
  2. Gently pull your head down at about a 45 degree angle, like you are pulling it towards your opposite knee until you feel a stretch.

Reps: 2-3 | Sets: 1-2 | Which Side: Both | Hold Time: 30 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: Levator, scapula, neck, shoulder, upper, back, stretch, DDD, herniated disc, scap, levator scap, levator scapula

description:
1. Put your arm straight out to the side about parallel to the ground. Turn your palm up and keep your fingers straight out. Side bend and rotate your head away like you are looking down at the floor.
  2.  At your wrist, move your hand up and down keeping your fingers straight.

Reps: 5-10 | Sets: 1-2 | Which Side: Both | Hold Time: 1-2 seconds | How Often: 1-2 times per day
tags: Median, nerve, glide, flossing, ROM, wrist, neck, TOS, thoracic outlet syndrome, DDD, herniated disc, impingement, carpal tunnel syndrome

description:
1. Stand in a doorway, and place your arm up on the doorway at about a 90-degree angle.
  2. Lean through the doorway until you feel a stretch.

Reps: 2-3 | Sets: 1-2 | Which Side: Both | Hold Time: 30 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: chest stretch, chest tightness, shoulder tightness, shoulder pain

description:
1. Sit in a chair that has a low back.
  2. Looking up towards the ceiling, bend backwards over the chair until you feel a stretch. Hold the stretch.

Reps: 3-5 | Sets: 1-2 | Hold Time: 10-15 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: back, stretch, ROM, neck, shoulder, TOS, thoracic outlet syndrome, text neck, upper back pain

description:
1. Start with your shoulders relaxed and your elbows slightly bent.
  2. Imagine there is a hand on your spine, and squeeze your shoulder blades back and together like you are trying to squeeze the hand.

Reps: 10-15 | Sets: 2-3 | Which Side: Both | Hold Time: 1-2 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: Shoulder, squeezes, ROM, strengthening, scapular, scapular squeeze, scapular squeezes

description:
1. Sitting in a chair, place your hand of the side you want to stretch underneath your thigh so you are sitting on it. You can also place it behind your back.
  2. Take your other hand over your head and place it on the side you want to stretch. Gently pull towards the other side until you feel a stretch, and hold.

Reps: 2-3 | Sets: 1-2 | Which Side: Both | Hold Time: 30 seconds | How Often: 2-3 times per day
tags: Upper, trapezius, neck, shoulder, stretch, back,  RTC, rotator cuff, shoulder impingement, shoulder bursitis, shoulder tendonitis, labral repair, trap, traps, trap stretch
